title: fix: no-play argument for API client
body:
Description:
- This PR fixes a bug where the --play flag has no effect because its default is True and the parser does not recognize a --play False value. By using argparse.BooleanOptionalAction, the CLI now automatically exposes --no-play, allowing explicit disabling of playback via the API client.

Compatibility:
- This change preserves the default behavior (playback remains enabled by default) but adds a new --no-play option.
- Note: argparse.BooleanOptionalAction is available in Python 3.9+. If the project must support older Python versions, a compatibility shim would be needed.

What to modify:
- Update the argument definition in tools/api_client.py to use BooleanOptionalAction for --play.
- No other behavioral changes are expected besides enabling the explicit --no-play flag.

If you encounter issues on older Python runtimes, consider a fallback implementation or a Python version constraint.

step_plan.summary: Enable explicit disabling of playback in the API client by replacing the --play flag with argparse.BooleanOptionalAction, preserving default playback while exposing --no-play.

steps.operation: Replace --play argument with BooleanOptionalAction in parse_args
steps.reason: BooleanOptionalAction provides automatic --no-play support, allowing explicit disabling of playback while preserving default True behavior
steps.side_effects: Requires Python 3.9+. Downstream code consuming args.play should continue to work as booleans; potential need to adjust tests to cover --no-play; may require updating runtime configuration for older Python versions
